item.title: Companies with Mythos access urge joint defence of infrastructure
item.description: Cyber security chiefs warn rollout of new tool will need close co-ordination between governments and businesses

item.title: EV ownership at ‘tipping point’ in many parts of the world, experts say
item.description: Data shows momentum shift under way in Europe and emerging markets

item.title: Defence stocks give back gains as investors buy rumour but sell war
item.description: Production bottlenecks and uncertainty over US munitions funding weigh on stock prices for weapons makers

item.description: Paris-based group in talks with its investors amid slump in high-end market

item.title: What the AI ‘jobpocalypse’ narrative misses
item.description: Whether or not new technologies can perform a task is only a tiny part of the picture

item.title: Iran seeks to resume flights from bomb-damaged airports
item.description: Authorities are reopening travel hubs after Tehran suspended air traffic because of US-Israeli strikes

item.title: Banks brace for tax raid if Starmer is ousted
item.description: Executives prepare for shift to the left in UK government after narrow escape in last year’s Budget

item.title: America’s energy supremacy is being forged in war
item.description: The US stands to benefit as war in Iran reshapes oil and gas flows, but Europe and Asia are wary of becoming too reliant on American supply

item.title: From Anthropic’s Mythos to the Birkin bag, scarcity sells
item.description: The idea that Mythos is too hot to handle ought to be good for the company’s valuation

item.title: The incredible double life of a spyware salesman turned spy
item.description: He built a booming business selling spy gadgets to the paranoid and the powerful. Behind the scenes he was wiring his own world

item.title: Can I choose my next car without crashing my finances?
item.description: James Max finds that picking his next vehicle has been the hardest in 40 years of driving

item.title: The billionaire MSC ‘captain’ handing over his fleet
item.description: Gianluigi Aponte built a $44bn fortune turning a single-vessel business into the world’s biggest container shipping group

item.description: Repair-friendly suitcases, watches, laptops and more

item.title: Investors push for higher yield on $14bn of Oracle-backed data centre debt
item.description: Huge bond offering comes amid concerns over tech giant’s debt load and recent flood of AI-related issuance

item.title: German regulator clamps down on UniCredit ads in Commerzbank takeover bid
item.description: BaFin has prohibited Italian lender from describing German bank as ‘neglected’, ‘unsafe’ or ‘short-term-oriented’

item.title: BA forced to downgrade thousands more loyalty scheme members after ‘error’
item.description: Some passengers were wrongly told they would keep their Gold or Silver tier status in shake-up of its loyalty programme

item.title: Witkoff and Kushner to travel to Islamabad as hopes rise for Iran peace talks
item.description: US delegation wants to ‘hear Iranians out’ as Tehran’s foreign minister also confirms trip to Pakistan’s capital

item.title: Boots hopes its upcoming IPO will be bigger, bolder, more beautiful
item.description: UK retailer has grown sales at a steady 6% annually over the past five years

item.title: Google to invest up to $40bn in Anthropic
item.description: Search giant increases its financial support to help the AI lab add computing power to run its models

item.title: AI data centre emissions vastly underestimated, UK admits
item.description: New projections raise forecasts of climate impact by up to 136 times

item.title: Trading firm Jane Street doubles revenues to $40bn
item.description: Record haul surpasses investment banks which once dominated Wall Street

item.title: Intel shares surge on AI boom to surpass dotcom bubble high
item.description: CEO says US chipmaker has made ‘fundamental’ changes after year-long turnaround

item.title: Amazon-backed nuclear developer X-energy surges in trading debut
item.description: Company taps rising demand for electricity from data centres to power AI boom

item.title: Private credit won’t spark the next financial crisis
item.description: A lower leverage ratio and limited links to banks mean that the sector is unlikely to pose a systemic threat

item.title: The Apple juggernaut and the AI roadblock
item.description: John Ternus will face innovation challenges that Tim Cook never had to

item.title: London’s luxury hoteliers lose sleep over fall in Middle East visitors
item.description: Expensive rooms go unsold as Iran conflict disrupts Gulf travel and dents demand from big-spending guests
